sentence: O ceu nun esta claru hoije.
ipa: u ˈsew ˈnũ ˈesta ˈklaɾu ˈojʒe
gloss_en: The sky is not clear today.
notes: Masc. sing. article 'o' (PT-type, the highest-confidence Guadramil isogloss vs Rionorese 'al') per frias_conde_sanabria; final -o>-u per ferreira1995.

sentence: Estes çapatos custan quarenta euros.
ipa: ˈestes saˈpatos kuˈstɐ̃ kwaˈɾẽta ˈewɾos
gloss_en: These shoes cost forty euros.
notes: seseo ç>[s] (merged with Galician [s], not Castilian [θ]) per vasconcelos1900 EPM II p.54; 3pl -an from the shared Leonese base per frias_conde_sanabria; nasal [ɐ̃] in 'custan' per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: La reunion ye manhana as tres da tarde.
ipa: la reˈwnjo ˈje maˈɲana ˈas ˈtɾes ˈda ˈtaɾde
gloss_en: The meeting is tomorrow at three in the afternoon.
notes: Leonese -on [õ] for PT -ão and copula 'ye' per frias_conde_sanabria; palatal <nh> [ɲ] per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: O miou armanu bibe perto do riu.
ipa: u ˈmjou aˈɾmanu ˈbiβe ˈpeɾtu ˈdu ˈrju
gloss_en: My brother lives near the river.
notes: Masc. article 'o'/'do' (Guadramil isogloss) per frias_conde_sanabria; betacism 'bibe' (< vive) and trill [r] 'riu' per penny2002.

sentence: Bai en frente i bira pela primeira rua.
ipa: ˈbaj ẽ ˈfɾẽte ˈi ˈbiɾa pela pɾiˈmejɾa ˈrwa
gloss_en: Go straight ahead and turn on the first street.
notes: Betacism 'bai'/'bira' (< vai/vira) per penny2002; retained <ei> 'primeira' and trill onset [r] per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: La sopa de peixe estaba mui saborosa.
ipa: la ˈs̺opa de ˈpejʃe eˈstaβa ˈmuj s̺aβoˈɾoz̺a
gloss_en: The fish soup was very tasty.
notes: Imperfect -aba and <ei> 'peixe'; no coda [ʃ] (Leonese [s]) per frias_conde_sanabria; open register per ferreira1995.

sentence: Adonde apanho o carru?
ipa: aˈðõde aˈpaɲu u ˈkaru
gloss_en: Where do I catch the cart?
notes: Masc. article 'o' + betacism-free onset per frias_conde_sanabria; palatal <nh> [ɲ] 'apanho' and trill [r] 'carru' per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Quantas personas benen a la cena?
ipa: ˈkwɐ̃tas peˈɾsonas beˈnẽ a la ˈsena
gloss_en: How many people are coming to supper?
notes: Betacism 'benen' (< vêm) and nasal [ɐ̃] 'quantas' per penny2002; feminine article 'la' retained (partial paradigm) per frias_conde_sanabria.

sentence: Se you podira, mercaba aquela casa.
ipa: s̺e ˈjow poˈðiɾa meˈɾkaβa aˈkela ˈkaz̺a
gloss_en: If I could, I would buy that house.
notes: 1sg pronoun 'you' [jow] (Guadramil vs Rionorese 'ieu') per frias_conde_sanabria; imperfect -aba per penny2002.

sentence: Diban a la feira mercar fruta i berças.
ipa: diˈβɐ̃ a la ˈfejɾa meˈɾkaɾ ˈfɾuta ˈi ˈbeɾsas
gloss_en: They used to go to the fair to buy fruit and greens.
notes: Imperfect 'diban' of 'ir' (leveled Leonese stem dir-, a Guadramil innovation) per frias_conde_sanabria; betacism 'berças' per penny2002.

sentence: Manhana bamos de barcu ata la ilha.
ipa: maˈɲana ˈbamos de ˈbaɾku ˈata la ˈiʎa
gloss_en: Tomorrow we go by boat to the island.
notes: Betacism 'bamos' (< vamos) per penny2002; palatal <lh> [ʎ] 'ilha' per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Tchama-me quando tchegares a casa.
ipa: ˈtʃama me ˈkwɐ̃du tʃeˈɡaɾes a ˈkaz̺a
gloss_en: Call me when you get home.
notes: Affricate <tch> [t͡ʃ] in 'tchamar' and native 'tchegares' (< plicare), inherited from the Rionorese base per frias_conde_sanabria; nasal [ɐ̃] 'quando' per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Buônas tardes, i como bos ba?
ipa: ˈbwɔnas ˈtaɾdes ˈi ˈkomu ˈbos ˈba
gloss_en: Good afternoon, and how are you?
notes: Diphthong <uô> [wɔ] 'buônas' per frias_conde_sanabria; betacism 'bos ba' (< vos vai) per penny2002.

sentence: Mais bal un paxaru na mano que dous a bolar.
ipa: ˈmajs ˈbal ũ paˈʃaɾu na ˈmanu ke ˈdows a boˈlaɾ
gloss_en: A bird in the hand is worth two in the bush.
notes: Betacism 'bal'/'bolar' and <x> [ʃ] 'paxaru' per penny2002; retained <ou> 'dous' per frias_conde_sanabria.

sentence: O gatu pretu duôrme al pie do lhar.
ipa: u ˈɡatu ˈpɾetu ˈdwɔɾme al ˈpje ˈdu ˈʎaɾ
gloss_en: The black cat sleeps by the hearth.
notes: Diphthong <uô> [wɔ] 'duôrme' and <ie> 'pie' per frias_conde_sanabria; masc. article 'o'/'do' per ferreira1995.

sentence: Aquel ratu fugiu para o buracu.
ipa: aˈkel ˈratu ˈfuxju ˈpaɾa u buˈɾaku
gloss_en: That mouse fled into the hole.
notes: Trill onset [r] 'ratu' and final -u per penny2002; masc. article 'o' per frias_conde_sanabria.

sentence: Todu o mundo fala deste assuntu.
ipa: ˈtoðu u ˈmũdu ˈfala ˈdeste aˈsũtu
gloss_en: The whole world talks about this matter.
notes: Final -o>-u ('todu','assuntu') and nasal [ũ] 'mundo' per frias_conde_sanabria; masc. article 'o' per ferreira1995.

sentence: Ye mui tchoco quando bebe.
ipa: ˈje ˈmuj ˈtʃoku ˈkwɐ̃du ˈbeβe
gloss_en: He is very dull when he drinks.
notes: Copula 'ye' and affricate <tch> [t͡ʃ] per frias_conde_sanabria; /b/ retained 'bebe' per penny2002.

sentence: Nel cuorpo lhieba una cuarda para o muarto.
ipa: nel ˈkwoɾpu ˈʎjeβa una ˈkwaɾda ˈpaɾa u ˈmwaɾtu
gloss_en: On the body he carries a cord for the dead man.
notes: Extreme Ŏ>/wo,wa/ 'cuorpo','cuarda','muarto' (the most extreme diphthong of the zone, Guadramil sub-zone) per frias_conde_sanabria, p.3; palatal <lh> 'lhieba' per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: O sol relumbra por riba de las lhombas.
ipa: u ˈs̺ol reˈlũbɾa poɾ ˈriβa de ˈlas ˈʎõbas
gloss_en: The sun shines over the hills.
notes: Coda /l/ 'sol' and trill onset [r] per mateus_dandrade2000; feminine plural 'las' retained per frias_conde_sanabria.
